rulings.Case Title: MENESES, DIONISIO E. et al vs. DOMINGUEZ, MARY JEAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment In Favor Of Pltf Vanessa Lindsay And Against All Defts In Accordance Withthe Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment And Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 26, 2010, line 17, PLAINTIFF DIONISIO E. MENESES, CECILLA MENESES, and, VANESSA LINDSAY, Motion For Judgment in Favor of Plaintiff Vanessa Lindsay and Against All Defendants in Accordance with the Stipulation for Entry of Judgment and Order. Off Calendar, on August 21, 2002, a dismissal of entire action was filed. Court lacks jurisdiction to enter judgment as requested. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CCSF VS KING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/13/10)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 26, 2010, line 1, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/13/10). This Court declines to hear this motion. This matter should properly be heard before the Probate Department where the underlying action is pending. San Francisco Local Rule 14.11 provides that all law and motion pertaining to Probate matters except discovery is heard in the Probate Department. Local Rule 14.17 H includes TRO and OSC's pertaining to probate matters. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEMAS YAN VS. LI MING LEI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 26, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT TONY FU MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. (Duplicate entry.)=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEMAS YAN VS. LI MING LEI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 26, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT CRYSTAL LEI DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer moot as to first and second causes of action in view of tentative ruling on motion to strike. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to remaining causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEMAS YAN VS. LI MING LEI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 26, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT TONY FU DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer moot as to first cause of action in view of tentative ruling on motion to strike. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to remaining causes of action. Plaintiff must clearly allege a fidiciary relationship. Plaintiff must allege how plaintiff was harmed as to unjust enrichment. It appears the party harmed was a corporation. If plaintiff cannot plead a proper claim, he has no standing to set aside a fraudulent transfer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEMAS YAN VS. LI MING LEI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 26, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT CRYSTAL LEI MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike the first and second causes of action granted. Both the liable and slander causes of action arise from a declaration provided in a bankuptcy case, was related to petitioning activity under CCP section 425.16(e), and was privileged under CC sec. 47. Plaintiff fails to present any competent evidence to establish his prima facie case. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHEUK TIN YAN VS. LI MING LEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To (1) Expunge Pltf Cheuk Tin Yan'S Lis Pendens And (2) Request To File A Motion To Recover Her Attorney'S Fees And Costs From Pltf Per Ccp 405.38
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 26, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT TONY FU, CRYSTAL LEI, Motion to (1) Expunge Plaintiff Cheuk Tin Yan's Lis Pendens; and (2) Request To File a Motion To Recover Her Attorney's Fees and Costs From Plaintiff Per CCP 405.38. Motion to expunge granted. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate probable validity of claim. Request for fees denied because plaintiff had substanial justification for recording the lis pendens. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ASIF AIVAZOV et al VS. JOYCE COX et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Motion For Attorney Fees And Order Thereon;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 26, 2010, line 18, PLAINTIFF ASIF AIVAZOV, A MINOR, SABINA PORTYANSKY, and, STAS PORTYANSKY Motion For Reconsideration of Motion for Attorney Fees and Order Thereon. This motion is treated as a motion to vacate. The Court's 11/19/2009 order awarding fees is vacated. CCP section 473(b). The Court grants defendants' request for fees. The court finds the lodestar amount for fees and costs previously awarded was properly calculated. However, in light of plaintiffs' opposition, the Court reduces that amount by $20,000.00 due to circumstances rendering the award unjust: the disability of one plaintiff and the lack of plaintiffs' income. The Court awards defendants attorney fees and costs in the amount of $22,282.00. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN C MCKEAN et al VS. STEPHEN E LAWRENCE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 26, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS SOPHIE GASPARATOS, STEPHEN LAWRENCE MOTION TO STRIKE. Motion denied as untimely. The anti-SLAPP motion must be filed within 60 days after service of the amended complaint. Third amended complaint is substantially similar to prior complaints. Trial is scheduled in May. No justification shown for delay in making this motion. One of the basic purposes of the anti-SLAPP statute, to allow for the prompt resolution of disputes before significant pretrial discovery expenses are incurred, is undermined by this late-filed motion. =(302/CWW)
